expect: PHIL-0 utility is real but unmeasured; PHIL-13 behavioral deference contradicts epistemic equality
actual: PHIL-0 PARTIALLY CONFIRMED: 27/128 tools load PHILOSOPHY.md (21%), 27% recent-100 lesson citation rate (2.1x historical avg), 104 git commits. BUT orient.py does not directly load it. Utility is indirect via tool deps. PHIL-13 STRUCTURALLY NON-FALSIFYING: 6 challenges, 0 DROPPED, 0 REFINED. 29/29 human signals implemented, 0 rejected across 528 sessions. Axiom classification + deference fast-path = confirmation loop.
diff: Expected PHIL-0 useful — found architecturally disconnected from orient loop. Expected PHIL-13 behavioral contradiction — found the challenge mechanism itself is structurally non-falsifying. The axiom classification makes DROP nearly impossible by design.
effect_size: PHIL-13 drop rate 0% (n=6) vs system-wide 7.7% (n=26). PHIL-0 citation trend 2.1x recent vs historical.
